name: Momentum Breakout Continuation
summary: Enter in the direction of an impulsive breakout after compression, pullback hold, or continuation from structure.
marketConditions: Directional session liquidity is present and range is expanding.
Price is breaking multi-candle balance or reclaiming a prior high or low and holding.
Body size and travel are above recent average while spread remains acceptable.

context: Mark the breakout base, recent swing, and liquidity target before entry.
Trade only in direction of the higher-timeframe impulse or fresh session expansion.
Wait for price to hold outside the broken range instead of chasing the first spike.
setup: Compression or pullback forms near the breakout level.
Retest shows shallow rejection and no deep close back inside range.
Order flow stays one-sided with consecutive closes in trend direction.
trigger: Enter on break of the retest high for longs or retest low for shorts.
Allow first continuation candle entry only if body closes beyond trigger level.
Skip entries if the trigger candle is already stretched more than one recent average range.
confirmation: Range expansion is above recent average.
Volume or speed increases through the trigger level.
Structure stays above reclaimed support for longs or below reclaimed resistance for shorts.
invalidation: Two-sided chop returns inside the prior range.
Retest closes back through the breakout base.
Session impulse fades and divergence becomes obvious.
noTrade: Major news hits within minutes of entry.
Breakout runs directly into higher-timeframe opposing structure.
Spread widens or the candle is mostly wick with no close-through.

stopLoss: Hide stop beyond retest low or high or behind the breakout base.
If structure is loose, use the far side of the compression box.
Risk must stay inside the planned per-trade limit.
takeProfit: Pay yourself at 1R around first extension or nearby liquidity.
Hold a runner toward session high, low, or measured move target.
Do not widen target after entry; trail only if structure earns it.
management: Move to break-even only after clean expansion and confirmed fresh structure.
Trail behind new 5m structure once price clears 1R.
Exit remainder if price re-enters balance and closes there.
timeExit: Flatten if continuation never appears within 3 to 6 execution candles.
Reduce size before low-liquidity session handoff.
Do not carry a scalp-style breakout into dead hours.
earlyExit: Full candle closes back inside range.
Counter impulse prints with larger body than the entry candle.
Breakout level fails on the next retest.

sizing: Size from stop distance, not conviction.
Reduce size on the second entry in the same session.
Avoid stacking multiple breakout trades on one symbol.
exposureLimits: No new trade if session loss cap is hit.
Cut risk in half after two failed breakouts in a row.

journaling: Record the balance range that broke.
Log whether entry was initial break or retest.
Review whether exit came from target, trail, or failed continuation.
assistantPrompts: What defines a valid breakout for you: body close, wick break, or retest hold?
How many candles are you willing to wait for continuation before exiting?
Which higher-timeframe level makes you cancel the setup?
